{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

## Risk estimation and prediction

::: notes
In Epi 203,
you have already seen
methods for modeling binary outcomes
using one covariate that is also binary
(such as exposure/non-exposure).
In this section, we review one-covariate analyses,
with a special focus on risk ratios and odds ratios,
which are important concepts for interpreting logistic regression.
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec_risk_estimation.qmd >}}

## Comparing probabilities

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec_compare_probs.qmd >}}

## Odds and odds ratios

### Odds and probabilities

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec_odds.qmd >}}

### The inverse odds function

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec_invodds.qmd >}}

### Odds ratios

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec_odds_ratios.qmd >}}

## The logit and expit functions

### The logit function

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec_logit.qmd >}}

### The expit function

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec_expit.qmd >}}

### Diagram of expit and logit {#sec-expit-logit-diagram}

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec_logistic-link-diagram.qmd >}}
